_publications._publicationTitle: Nf1 mutant mice with p19ARF gene loss develop accelerated hematopoietic disease resembling acute leukemia with a variable phenotype.
_publications._context: Mx1-Cre, Nf1flox, and Nf1fcr (Nf1 knockout) mice have been described previously | The Nf1 flox/- mice are in a mixed genetic background of C57BL/6/ Sv129 and have been described previously | The Nf1flox and Prx1Cre lines were maintained by continuous backcrossing to wild-type C57BL/6J mice | The female Nf1flox mice were crossed to male Nf1flox heterozygous Prx1-Cre-positive males

_publications._publicationTitle: Cell of origin and microenvironment contribution for NF1-associated dermal neurofibromas.
Modelling neurofibromatosis type 1 tibial dysplasia and its treatment with lovastatin.
MIA is a potential biomarker for tumour load in neurofibromatosis type 1.
